title: atomic-skills repo routing + integration config (R-XAGENT-10 / R-EXEC-41)
description: Validates the repo's operator-supplied routing and integration config at `.atomic-skills/status/routing.json`. This config is the ONLY surface that enables Mode 2 (Opus-plans / other-model-executes); the implement skill reads it and declares per-task tier REQUIREMENTS + the dispatch CONTRACT only — never the provider economics or cost narrative (those live here, operator-owned). ABSENT FILE ⇒ Mode-1-only defaults (mode2Enabled=false): Mode 1 self-exec is the unconditional default. An empty object `{}` is valid and means all defaults. `$/token` framing is an explicit non-goal and has no field here by design.

mode2Enabled.description: Master switch. Default (and absent-file behaviour) is false ⇒ Mode-1-only (the shipped default, safe for a fresh install with no Codex configured). When set true the Codex lane is ON and is the DEFAULT executor for spec-ready, verifier-bearing tasks (R-EXEC-44: enabling is explicit + file-backed, never implicit; the operator opts a batch OUT to Mode 1, not in).

integrationRef.description: Repo-global integration ref where feature PRs land (for example, `develop`). Optional; when absent, the resolver (F1/T-002) applies the `develop` default and signals not-configured for the lazy prompt. The schema only validates shape and does not apply the default itself (Ajv here runs without useDefaults). Never per-plan.

tierMap.description: Per-task executor tier → model. Opus is NEVER an executor target (it PLANS/REVIEWS/judges only, R-EXEC-18/19/53); a value naming an Opus model is rejected. Codex is a cross-provider lane (see codexLane), not a tier rung.

codexLane.description: The cross-provider OpenAI/Codex workspace-write lane (R-EXEC-35) — the only lane that takes work entirely off the Claude account (lever 2) and the sole Mode-2 lane on Gemini.

timeoutSeconds.description: Passed to the portable run_with_timeout wrapper (124+142 ⇒ timeout, perl-alarm macOS fallback).

sandbox.description: Locked. The lane flips read-only→workspace-write and changes NOTHING else from invocation-canonical.txt; -a never / no --yolo / no --full-auto stay verbatim and are not configurable here.

minBatchTasks.description: Firing floor K (R-EXEC-32): the Mode-2 gate does not even fire below this many dispatch-eligible tasks.

requireDeterministicVerifier.description: F2 hard disqualifier (R-EXEC-33): every task must carry a deterministic kind:test/kind:shell verifier. Default true; setting false is unsupported for v1 (the cascade economics depend on a near-free auto-running judge).

ideOverrides.description: Host-specific lane availability (R-EXEC-36). On Gemini the investigator tool is likely read-only ⇒ the subagent executor lane collapses ⇒ Mode 2 = Codex-only.

subagentExecutor.description: Whether an in-tree subagent executor lane is dispatchable on Gemini. Default false (Codex-only on Gemini).

not.$comment: Opus must never be an executor target (R-EXEC-41 acceptance). Reject any model name containing 'opus' in any case.
